metadata.description: Stride task management skills for AI agents

plugins.name: stride
plugins.description: Drive the full Stride kanban task lifecycle from Claude Code — claiming, completing, creating and enriching tasks and goals, workflow orchestration, subagent dispatch, and automatic lifecycle hook execution.

plugins.name: stride-security-review
plugins.description: AI-powered security review via /stride-security-review:security-review. Two scan modes: diff (default, PR-gating) and --full (codebase posture check, batched with cross-batch dedup and skipped-files reporting). Detects 10 universal vulnerability classes plus 5 MAESTRO-derived agentic-AI classes (prompt injection, tool abuse, agent trust boundary, model output execution, vector-store poisoning). Seven framework rule packs (new in 2.3.0): Android/Kotlin (exported components, WebView JS bridge RCE, cleartext traffic, SharedPreferences credentials, SQLite injection, trust-all SSL), Django/Python (mark_safe, raw/extra SQL, CSRF, production settings, mass-assignment, open-redirect, deserialization, SSRF, DRF mass-assignment), Express/Node.js (reflected XSS, eval RCE, child_process injection, Mongoose NoSQL injection, prototype pollution), iOS/Swift (UserDefaults credentials, WKWebView file access, ATS arbitrary loads, URLSession trust-all, deep-link missing auth), Phoenix/Elixir (raw/1, force_ssl, CSRF, fragment interpolation, LiveView scope, mass-assignment, Token replay, System.cmd shell, LiveView upload guards, open-redirect), Rails/Ruby (html_safe, find_by_sql, CSRF, params.permit!, eval/send, open-redirect, deserialization, connection.execute, missing-auth, JSON-leak), React/Next.js (dangerouslySetInnerHTML, API route missing auth, redirect open-redirect, javascript: href scheme, getServerSideProps secret leak). Plus a multi-platform CI/CD pipeline pack (8 platforms) and a framework-agnostic Web defense-in-depth pack covering security headers (CSP/HSTS/X-Frame-Options/cookie flags). Every finding carries CWE-ID and OWASP Top 10 references. Opt-in flags: --maestro (7-layer threat classification with explicit non-AI mapping), --rci [N] (recursive criticism passes), --baseline (suppress acknowledged findings), --patches (surgical-fix unified diffs), --fail-on <severity> (CI exit-code gate), --base <ref> (PR-against-base diff scope), --sarif (SARIF v2.1.0 output for GitHub Code Scanning). Ships a reference GitHub Actions workflow and a TAP 13 eval runner over 70 fixtures with positive-and-negative-control coverage, plus golden-file tests for the deterministic SARIF/dedup/fingerprint/fail-on transforms (new in 2.4.0). Semantic analysis, not pattern matching. Loosely based on anthropics/claude-code-security-review.

plugins.name: stride-ideation
plugins.description: Turn a fuzzy idea into shipped Stride tasks via two slash commands — /ideate drives an interactive, profile-aware question loop that hard-gates seven requirements sections into a committed markdown doc, and /stridify decomposes that doc into Stride goals and tasks via the batch API.

plugins.name: stride-lite
plugins.description: Lightweight companion plugin to Stride — produces Stride-shaped goal and task markdown documents on disk from a free-text prompt plus an optional requirements directory. No API calls, no kanban setup, no auth files. Just markdown. Useful when you want the Stride field discipline (acceptance criteria, key files, pitfalls, testing strategy, dependencies) without committing to the full Stride server workflow — the documents it produces can be reviewed, edited, or later submitted to a real Stride deployment by hand or by tooling, but this plugin never does so itself. Surface: three slash commands and one workflow skill. /stride-lite:create-goal decomposes a prompt into a goal directory with one goal.md plus 1–8 child taskN.md files; /stride-lite:create-task renders a single task markdown file; /stride-lite:init scaffolds a project-local .stride_lite.md with email plus three hook sections (## before_task, ## after_task, ## after_goal). The stride-lite-workflow skill is the file-based equivalent of the full Stride plugin's stride-workflow — it walks a goal directory through an eight-step task lifecycle (select → before_task → explore → implement → after_task → review → review-loop decision → completion + archive), dispatching the two read-only subagents at the right moments. Three subagents are shipped: stride-lite:create-decomposer (decomposes a free-text prompt into a goal + child tasks YAML, no API calls), stride-lite:task-explorer (read-only enrichment with codebase context — Read / Grep / Glob, no Bash, no WebFetch; appends ## Exploration Report), and stride-lite:task-reviewer (reviews changes against acceptance criteria, pitfalls, patterns, and testing strategy with narrowly-scoped read-only git Bash; appends ## Review Report with embedded reviewer_result JSON, schema 1.1). v0.9.0+: hooks/hooks.json registers Claude Code PreToolUse/PostToolUse handlers that auto-fire the three .stride_lite.md sections at the corresponding workflow intercepts — ## before_task before the task-explorer Agent dispatch (blocking, exit 2 stops the dispatch), ## after_task before the task-reviewer Agent dispatch (blocking, exit 2 stops the dispatch), ## after_goal after the goal.md Edit/Write that appends ## Completion Summary (advisory — PostToolUse cannot roll back the write). Cross-platform from day one: hooks/stride-lite-hook.sh (POSIX bash, pure-bash JSON parsing, no jq dependency) and hooks/stride-lite-hook.ps1 (PowerShell 5.1+, ConvertFrom-Json / ConvertTo-Json, no module installs); the .sh script auto-delegates to .ps1 on native Windows (OSTYPE unset + COMSPEC set). v0.10.0+: stride-lite-workflow Step 8 final-task branch performs a terminal PENDING → IMPLEMENTED archive move — after ## after_goal fires, the goal directory moves from docs/implementation/PENDING/<slug>/ to docs/implementation/IMPLEMENTED/<slug>/ via git mv when tracked (preserves rename history) or plain mv otherwise. Collision suffixing on the target with -2 / -3 / … up to a 1000-iteration safety cap (never overwrites), after-goal-failure no-move guard (a structured failed-status JSON from the harness skips the move so the user can re-trigger), non-/PENDING/ path skip with a stderr warning (custom --output-dir is left where it is), and clean exit on filesystem-mv failure (logged, the workflow does not fail because of an archive-move failure). Single-task files under PENDING/tasks/ are NEVER moved — only goal directories. v0.11.0+: copy installs ship the hook enforcement layer — install.sh previously copied every directory except hooks/, so script-based installs silently lacked the documented auto-fire entirely (pre-0.11.0 copy installs should re-run ./install.sh --force); the installer now copies hooks/ with the executable bit preserved, reports it in the summary, and fails loudly if hooks.json does not land. Also: the init surface, workflow walkthrough, and scaffolded template all teach the v0.9.0 harness-executes model (the stale static-config and workflow-skill-executes claims are gone), phantom /stride-lite:ideate//stride-lite:decompose references are purged with the decomposer's unjustified Read/Grep grant removed, the smoke suite grows 24 to 43 assertions with machine-enforced init-template parity and nine hook-routing payload fixtures, and the shipped init-command goal artifact is archived PENDING to IMPLEMENTED per the plugin's own convention.

plugins.name: launchdarkly
plugins.description: Generate correct, testable, and removable LaunchDarkly feature-flag code from Claude Code — covers the Java server SDK and the TypeScript SDKs (Node server, client-side JS, React) with skills, scaffolding and flag-removal commands, and an anti-pattern review agent.
